toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. For .NET, we can use the `--crashdump` option or configure crash dump collection via a `.runsettings` file or command-line arguments.

Since this project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, it uses the newer Microsoft.Testing.Platform. With the newer testing platform, crash dump collection can be enabled via `--crashdump` flag.

However, looking at the older approach, we could also use the `blame-crash` data collector: `dotnet test --blame-crash`.

Since `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is `true`, this project uses Microsoft Testing Platform integration. 

Even though the project uses the newer testing platform, `dotnet test` still acts as a wrapper, so the blame-crash collector should work fine. I'll go ahead and run the tests with the crash dump option enabled.

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-44ae295d757f4d4f8bed3ca8508fe86d && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The command to run tests with crash dump collection is:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

This uses the **blame-crash** data collector, which monitors the test host process and produces a crash dump (`.dmp` file) if it terminates unexpectedly. The dump will be saved in the `TestResults` directory if a crash occurs.
